title: Peekable<Chars> single-char lookahead for block comments is correct
description: The block comment handler in lexer.rs (lines 68-96) consumes '/' via advance(), then peeks for '*' — one character of lookahead. The '*/' terminator detection (line 83) reads a char via advance(), then peeks for '/' — again one character of lookahead. Peekable<Chars> provides exactly one character of lookahead, and the code uses it correctly at each decision point without ever needing to look two characters ahead simultaneously. A reviewer might suggest a sliding-window or multi-peek approach, but the existing step-by-step approach is correct, idiomatic, and does not require any additional lookahead beyond what Peekable provides.
